biologyPathway.title: Muscle Guarding
biologyPathway.detail: Neck muscles can tighten from stress, posture, strain, injury, or pain, reducing motion.

biologyPathway.title: Spine/Nerve Causes
biologyPathway.short: Discs, joints, and nerves can contribute.
biologyPathway.detail: Cervical disc disease, arthritis, stenosis, or nerve compression can cause neck pain with arm symptoms.

biologyPathway.title: Infection Red Flags
biologyPathway.short: Fever plus stiff neck can be serious.
biologyPathway.detail: Meningitis and other infections can include fever, headache, stiff neck, confusion, rash, or light sensitivity.

biologyPathway.title: Safe Movement
biologyPathway.detail: Gentle mobility, ergonomics, PT, heat/ice, and medical review can help depending on cause. Yanking the neck is caveman medicine.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Stiff Neck Is a Symptom
body: Neck stiffness can be muscle tension, posture, strain, arthritis, disc disease, or nerve irritation.
It can also be infection or trauma-related, because the neck insists on being important.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Movement Narrows
body: Tight muscles and irritated joints can reduce rotation, flexion, extension, and comfort.
Shoulders, jaw, sleep position, screens, and stress all enjoy joining the committee.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Red Flags
body: Fever, severe headache, stiff neck, rash, confusion, light sensitivity, trauma, weakness, numbness, or bowel/bladder symptoms need urgent care.
A possible meningitis or spinal cord sign is not a stretching prompt.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Support Plan
body: Care may include PT, ergonomics, heat/ice, medication, posture/load changes, imaging, or urgent treatment depending on cause.
The steering wheel needs safe unlocking, not heroic cranking.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Locked Steering Wheel
paragraphs: Your neck is a steering wheel locked straight ahead.
The driver says we're fine, while the car misses every exit and becomes emotionally attached to tunnel vision.
Sometimes the lock is stress. Sometimes posture. Sometimes disc, nerve, arthritis, trauma, or infection.
Unlocking it safely means checking red flags, reducing load, and restoring motion without trying to wrench the whole dashboard off.
Unlock the steering wheel safely or keep driving straight into the same wall. Your choice.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Refusal to Look Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have a neck stiffness problem. You may also have a refusal-to-look pattern.
Medical first: stiff neck with fever, severe headache, neurologic symptoms, trauma, or rash needs care.
Symbolically, ask what you refuse to turn toward.
But do not confuse meningitis red flags with emotional resistance. The neck gets triage before poetry.
Unlock the steering wheel safely or keep driving straight into the same wall. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Bladder-Gallbladder Stagnation
paragraphs: In TCM imagery, neck/shoulder stiffness maps to blocked flow through related channels.
Medical red flags still get priority.

influenceLayers.title: Perspective Gate
paragraphs: The neck lets the head see more than one direction.
Stiffness can symbolize narrowed perspective.

influenceLayers.title: Locked Wheel
paragraphs: The locked wheel symbolizes fixation.
Safe turning returns choice.

influenceLayers.title: Head-Body Bridge
paragraphs: The neck connects thinking with action.
Tension here often carries both physical and emotional load.

influenceLayers.title: Look Safely
paragraphs: The reset is gentle motion, care, and perspective.
No forced cranking. We evolved past that, allegedly.

reset.title: Steering Wheel Unlock & Cervical Release
steps: Sit upright with the neck supported.
Place one hand on the back of the neck and one on the heart.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I turn safely. I allow perspective without force.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Chronic Neck Stiffness may require medical evaluation, diagnosis, and condition-specific treatment.

items.label: Gentle mobility
items.detail: Use slow, pain-free range only. No aggressive twisting.

items.label: Heat/ice
items.detail: Heat may help chronic tension; ice may help acute irritation.

items.label: Ergonomics
items.detail: Screens, pillows, driving, and work posture can matter.

items.label: PT/medical care
items.detail: Persistent, recurrent, neurologic, or severe symptoms deserve evaluation.

avoid: Do not ignore fever, severe headache, rash, light sensitivity, trauma, weakness, numbness, bowel/bladder changes, or severe worsening pain.
Do not force neck cracking or aggressive stretches.

brutalActions: Turn toward one truth without cranking the steering wheel off.

clinical.mechanism: Chronic neck stiffness is reduced neck range of motion, pain, tightness, or guarding. Causes can include muscle strain, posture, stress tension, arthritis, disc disease, nerve compression, whiplash, spinal stenosis, infection, meningitis, inflammatory disease, or trauma.
protocol: Track fever, headache, rash, light sensitivity, trauma, neurologic symptoms, arm numbness/weakness, bowel/bladder changes, severe pain, night pain, stiffness duration, posture triggers, and range of motion.
Use medical evaluation when symptoms are new, severe, progressive, persistent, recurrent, or paired with red flags.
Symbolic interpretation never replaces diagnosis or treatment.

hero.primaryPattern: Sciatica is radiating nerve pain down the buttock/leg pathway and may involve disc herniation, stenosis, nerve irritation, piriformis/deep gluteal compression, trauma, or other causes.
hero.safetyPreview: Bladder/bowel changes, groin/saddle numbness, progressive weakness, fever, trauma, cancer/infection risk, or severe unrelenting pain needs urgent care.

biologyPathway.title: Radiating Nerve Pain
biologyPathway.short: Pain travels down the nerve pathway.
biologyPathway.detail: Sciatica can cause sharp, burning, shooting, electric pain with numbness, tingling, or weakness.

biologyPathway.title: Common Causes
biologyPathway.short: Disc and spine causes are common.
biologyPathway.detail: Herniated disc, stenosis, spondylolisthesis, degenerative changes, or deep gluteal/piriformis compression may irritate the nerve.

biologyPathway.title: Red Flags
biologyPathway.detail: Bladder/bowel dysfunction, saddle numbness, progressive weakness, fever, trauma, or severe neurologic symptoms need prompt care.

biologyPathway.title: Movement Plan
biologyPathway.detail: Care may include physical therapy, meds, imaging if indicated, injections, or surgery for severe/progressive cases.

clinical.label: Strictly Medical
clinical.title: Sciatic Nerve Irritation & Radiating Pain Cascade

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Symptom, Not Diagnosis
body: Sciatica describes radiating pain along the sciatic nerve pathway.
The cause still has to be found, because nerves love mystery novels.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Nerve Irritation
body: Irritated or compressed nerve roots can create pain, numbness, tingling, burning, or weakness down the leg.
Sitting, coughing, sneezing, or bending may worsen symptoms.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Causes
body: Herniated disc, spinal stenosis, degenerative changes, spondylolisthesis, deep gluteal compression, trauma, tumors, or infection can contribute.
The plan depends on the cause and severity.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Emergency Check
body: Seek urgent care for bladder/bowel changes, saddle/groin numbness, progressive leg weakness, fever, trauma, cancer/infection risk, or severe unrelenting pain.
Do not meditate through a possible nerve emergency.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Pinched Wire
paragraphs: Your sciatic nerve is an electrical wire running from the back into the leg.
Something is pinching the wire, and the lights are flickering all the way down the hallway.
The jolt is the wire screaming. The burn is the afterparty nobody invited.
Maybe the rock is a disc. Maybe stenosis. Maybe a cranky muscle. Maybe something more serious.
Move the rock safely. Do not kick the breaker box with stretching heroics.
Move the rock safely or keep letting the wire scream down your leg. Your choice.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Blocked Path Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have sciatica symptoms. You may also have a blocked-path pattern.
Medical first: radiating nerve pain needs evaluation when severe, persistent, weak, numb, or paired with red flags.
Symbolically, ask what path feels blocked.
But if your leg is weak or your bladder/bowels change, the symbolism can shut up and let emergency medicine drive.
Clear the path safely. Strengthen what supports it. Stop forcing pain into a personality contest.
Move the rock safely or keep letting the wire scream down your leg. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Kidney-Bladder Channel
paragraphs: In TCM imagery, the back and posterior leg map to Kidney/Bladder pathways.
Sciatica becomes a symbol of blocked downward flow.

influenceLayers.title: Stuck Self
paragraphs: The Stuck Self fears the next step.
The Mover clears the path with support.

influenceLayers.title: Pinched Wire
paragraphs: The wire image is nerve signaling under pressure.
Less compression, more signal. Basic electricity, except meat.

influenceLayers.title: Stand or Sit
paragraphs: Sciatica can make both motion and stillness difficult.
The body asks for the right movement, not random suffering.

influenceLayers.title: Clear the Path
paragraphs: The reset is red-flag screening, safe movement, and care.
Do not force the nerve. Nerves are petty.

reset.title: Sciatic Signal & Clear Path Reset
steps: Sit or lie in a position that does not worsen symptoms.
Place one hand on the lower back and one on the thigh.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I move safely. I allow the signal to calm.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Sciatica may require medical evaluation, diagnosis, and condition-specific treatment.

items.label: Red flag screen
items.detail: Check bladder/bowel, saddle numbness, progressive weakness, fever, trauma, and severe pain first.

items.label: Relative rest
items.detail: Avoid positions/lifts that sharply worsen symptoms while staying gently mobile when safe.

items.label: Physical therapy
items.detail: Guided mobility, core/hip strength, and nerve-friendly movement may help.

items.label: Medical options
items.detail: Medications, imaging, injections, or surgery may be considered depending on cause/severity.

avoid: Do not ignore bladder/bowel changes, groin numbness, progressive weakness, fever, trauma, or severe worsening pain.
Do not force stretches that increase numbness, weakness, or radiating pain.

brutalActions: Move one real obstacle without abusing your nerve.

clinical.mechanism: Sciatica describes pain radiating along the sciatic nerve pathway, often from lower back or nerve root irritation/compression. Causes can include herniated disc, spinal stenosis, spondylolisthesis, piriformis/deep gluteal syndrome, degenerative changes, trauma, tumors, infection, or other spine/nerve issues.
protocol: Track pain path, numbness/tingling, weakness, bowel/bladder changes, saddle numbness, fever, trauma, cancer history, infection risk, sitting tolerance, cough/sneeze worsening, and walking limits.
Use symptom tracking and medical evaluation when symptoms are severe, persistent, recurrent, progressive, or paired with red flags.
Do not replace diagnosis or prescribed treatment with symbolic interpretation.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for medical care.

witty.metaphorText: The sciatic wire is pinched and the leg lights are flickering like a haunted hallway.
witty.wittyAdvice: Move the rock carefully. Do not kick the breaker box.

hero.primaryPattern: Scoliosis is a structural spinal curve with possible rotation; monitoring depends on age, curve size, growth, progression, pain, function, neurologic signs, and breathing impact.
hero.safetyPreview: Progressive curve, severe pain, neurologic symptoms, breathing trouble, bowel/bladder symptoms, or rapidly worsening posture needs medical care.

biologyPathway.title: Spinal Curve
biologyPathway.detail: Scoliosis involves lateral curvature and often rotation, measured clinically and radiographically.

biologyPathway.title: Growth and Progression
biologyPathway.detail: Adolescent curves may progress during growth; adult curves can be degenerative or related to prior curves.

biologyPathway.title: Symptoms
biologyPathway.detail: Some people have visible asymmetry without pain; others develop back pain, imbalance, or in severe cases breathing limits.

biologyPathway.title: Treatment Paths
biologyPathway.detail: Care depends on curve size, age, progression, symptoms, and risk. Naturally, the spine made it complicated.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Structural Curve
body: Scoliosis is a structural side-to-side curve, often with rotation.
It is not just bad posture, though posture can influence comfort.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Measurement
body: Curves are commonly tracked with imaging and measurements like Cobb angle.
Progression risk depends on growth, curve size, type, and age.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Treatment Options
body: Options can include observation, scoliosis-specific physical therapy, bracing during growth, pain management, or surgery for severe/progressive cases.
The spine, being a diva, prefers individualized engineering.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: Seek care for progressive curve, severe pain, neurologic symptoms, weakness/numbness, breathing trouble, or bowel/bladder changes.
A leaning tower still deserves an inspection.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Leaning Tower
paragraphs: Your spine is a leaning tower with one side carrying more load than the other.
The foundation may be fine, or it may need serious engineering review.
The builders keep saying we'll straighten it later, which is a bold management plan for architecture.
Sometimes it needs monitoring. Sometimes bracing. Sometimes PT. Sometimes surgery.
Balance is not pretending the curve is fake. Balance is supporting the structure that exists.
Balance the tower support or keep asking a leaning structure to pretend gravity is optional. Your choice.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Uneven Load Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have scoliosis. You may also have an uneven-load pattern.
Medical first: scoliosis is structural and needs appropriate monitoring, especially with progression, pain, growth, neurologic signs, or breathing symptoms.
Symbolically, ask where life became one-sided.
What burden keeps pulling you off center?
You can seek center without denying the curve.
Balance the tower support or keep asking a leaning structure to pretend gravity is optional. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Central Axis
paragraphs: The spine is the body's central pillar.
Scoliosis symbolically asks where the axis has been pulled.

influenceLayers.title: Uneven Load
paragraphs: One-sided burden becomes the symbolic language.
Structural care still matters more than poetic symmetry.

influenceLayers.title: Leaning Tower
paragraphs: The tower needs support, not shame.
Measurement beats guessing. Annoying, but true.

influenceLayers.title: Center Finder
paragraphs: Center is not always straight.
Center can mean supported alignment.

influenceLayers.title: Balanced Support
paragraphs: The reset is monitoring, strengthening, breath, and support.
The tower deserves scaffolding when needed.

reset.title: Centering & Tower Support Reset
steps: Sit or stand in a supported position.
Place one hand on the curve area and one on the opposite side.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I find support. I release uneven load where I can.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Scoliosis may require medical evaluation, diagnosis, and condition-specific treatment.

items.label: Monitoring
items.detail: Follow clinician guidance for imaging, Cobb angle tracking, and progression risk.

items.label: Scoliosis-informed PT
items.detail: Specialized exercises may support posture, breathing, strength, and comfort.

items.label: Bracing when indicated
items.detail: In growing adolescents, bracing may reduce progression risk for certain curves.

items.label: Pain/function support
items.detail: Core/hip strength, mobility, and ergonomic support may help symptoms.

avoid: Do not ignore progressive curvature, neurologic symptoms, breathing trouble, severe pain, or bowel/bladder symptoms.
Do not rely on generic posture advice for a structural curve.

brutalActions: Stop carrying one-sided loads when possible.
Ask the tower what scaffolding it needs.

clinical.mechanism: Scoliosis is a side-to-side spinal curvature, often with vertebral rotation. It may be idiopathic, congenital, neuromuscular, adolescent, or adult degenerative. Severity is monitored by exam and imaging such as Cobb angle.
protocol: Track curve monitoring, age/growth status, pain, uneven shoulders/hips, rib prominence, breathing symptoms, neurologic symptoms, progression, bracing/surgery history, and physical therapy plan.
Use symptom tracking and medical evaluation when symptoms are severe, persistent, recurrent, progressive, or paired with red flags.
Do not replace diagnosis or prescribed treatment with symbolic interpretation.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for medical care.

witty.metaphorText: The leaning tower is still standing, but someone should probably stop stacking emotional bricks on one side.
witty.wittyAdvice: Measure the curve. Support the tower. Gravity is rude but consistent.

Severe back pain, neurologic symptoms, weakness, numbness, or gait changes

hero.safetyPreview: Trauma, deformity, inability to lift arm, sudden weakness, severe pain, fever/redness, chest pain, or neurologic symptoms needs care.

biologyPathway.title: Mobile Joint
biologyPathway.short: The shoulder trades stability for range.
biologyPathway.detail: The shoulder moves a lot, which is lovely until tendons, capsule, bursa, or labrum start filing complaints.

biologyPathway.title: Rotator Cuff/Bursa
biologyPathway.detail: Rotator cuff injury, impingement, or bursitis often hurts with reaching, lifting, overhead activity, or lying on that side.

biologyPathway.title: Frozen Shoulder
biologyPathway.detail: Adhesive capsulitis causes progressive pain and stiffness and is associated with diabetes and thyroid disease.

biologyPathway.title: Urgent Causes
biologyPathway.detail: Dislocation, fracture, infection, neurologic injury, or acute tear need proper evaluation.

clinical.label: Strictly Medical
clinical.title: Shoulder Mobility, Tendon, Bursa & Capsule Cascade

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Many Possible Causes
body: Shoulder pain is a symptom, not a diagnosis.
Causes include rotator cuff injury, bursitis, impingement, frozen shoulder, arthritis, instability, fracture, dislocation, labral injury, or neck referral.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Reach Gets Restricted
body: Pain may limit overhead use, sleep, lifting, dressing, or reaching behind the back.
The crane is built for range, not endless unpaid overtime.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Frozen Shoulder Pattern
body: Adhesive capsulitis can cause progressive stiffness and long recovery.
Diabetes and thyroid disease are common context clues.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: Seek urgent care for trauma, deformity, inability to move the arm, severe sudden weakness, fever/redness, chest pain, or neurologic symptoms.
Not every shoulder pain is 'slept wrong.' Some shoulders bring paperwork.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Overloaded Crane
paragraphs: Your shoulder is a construction crane with a suspicious maintenance record.
The rotator cuff is the cable. The bursa is the pulley. The capsule is the crane housing that can freeze like bad bureaucracy.
One more lift, says the operator. One more lift, says the body, while the cable quietly frays.
Sometimes it needs PT. Sometimes imaging. Sometimes injection or surgery. Sometimes a diabetes/thyroid check.
The crane can recover better when you stop making it carry the emotional furniture too.
Unload the crane properly or keep making the shoulder carry a sky it never ordered. Your choice.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Weight You're Still Carrying Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have a shoulder problem. You may also have a carrying-too-much pattern.
Medical first: shoulder pain can be tendon, bursa, capsule, joint, nerve, fracture, dislocation, or referred pain.
Symbolically, ask what weight you keep shouldering.
What reach is costing you? What burden became identity?
Put it down emotionally. Also get care if the arm is weak, frozen, deformed, traumatic, or not improving.
Unload the crane properly or keep making the shoulder carry a sky it never ordered. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Lung-Large Intestine
paragraphs: In TCM imagery, the shoulder can relate to grief, breath, and release.
Letting go does not replace rotator cuff rehab. Obviously.

influenceLayers.title: Atlas Complex
paragraphs: The Atlas Complex carries the sky.
The Surrendered One puts down what is not theirs.

influenceLayers.title: Overloaded Crane
paragraphs: The crane image asks what has exceeded safe load.
Cables deserve maintenance. So do humans, apparently.

influenceLayers.title: Put It Down

The shoulder is not a storage rack.

Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.

biologyPathway.title: Disc Cushion
biologyPathway.detail: A disc has a tougher outer layer and softer inner material that helps absorb pressure.

biologyPathway.title: Herniation
biologyPathway.detail: Disc material can irritate or compress nearby nerves, causing local and radiating symptoms.

biologyPathway.title: Nerve Symptoms
biologyPathway.detail: Lumbar discs can cause sciatica-like leg pain; cervical discs can radiate into the arm.

biologyPathway.title: Emergency Signs

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Disc Material Under Pressure
body: A herniated disc happens when disc material bulges or leaks through the outer layer.
Sometimes it causes pain; sometimes imaging looks dramatic and symptoms are not. Bodies, naturally, are inconsistent.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Nerve Irritation
body: Nearby nerve roots can become irritated or compressed.
This can cause numbness, tingling, burning, weakness, or radiating pain down an arm or leg.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Treatment Options
body: Care may include physical therapy, medication, activity modification, injections, imaging, and sometimes surgery.
Core/hip strength, lifting mechanics, and graded recovery matter when safe.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Emergency Check

A nerve emergency is not a time to become a wellness influencer.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Overstuffed Cushion
paragraphs: Your spine is an overstuffed couch and the disc is the cushion.
Too much load, twist, strain, degeneration, or bad luck, and the cushion bulges like it has had enough of your household.
Then the filling presses on the wiring. The wiring is the nerve. The wiring objects loudly.
The fix may be PT, meds, imaging, injections, surgery, or simply not re-enacting a warehouse job during recovery.
Pressure needs redistribution. Limits need respect.
Take weight off the couch or keep waiting for the cushion to file bankruptcy. Your choice.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest

paragraphs: You may have a disc problem. You may also have an overload-and-limit pattern.

Symbolically, ask what pressure has exceeded capacity.
What have you carried past the point of structural honesty?
Set limits. Rehab smart. Do not make your spine negotiate with your pride.
Take weight off the couch or keep waiting for the cushion to file bankruptcy. Your choice.

Chest pain, shortness of breath, jaw/arm pain, sweating, nausea, or left upper back pain with cardiac symptoms
Severe abdominal pain, urinary symptoms, flank pain, blood in urine, gallbladder/liver-type pain, or referred organ concern
Right upper back/shoulder pain with right upper abdominal pain, vomiting, fever, jaundice, or fatty-meal trigger may need gallbladder/liver evaluation
seek_immediate_care_if: Pain follows trauma, comes with bowel/bladder changes, saddle numbness, new weakness/numbness, chest symptoms, severe abdominal/flank pain, fever/infection signs, or severe sudden worsening
schedule_care_if: Pain persists beyond a week or two, worsens, limits function, radiates, recurs, appears after age 50, or has concerning medical history
medical_safety.self_care_allowed_if: Gentle movement, posture changes, heat/ice, rest from aggravating activity, and reflection may support mild non-emergency back pain.
medical_safety.do_not_ignore: Back pain can be mechanical, nerve-related, organ-referred, infection-related, cancer-related, or cardiac/lung-related depending on location and symptoms.
medical_safety.disclaimer: This app is for education and self-reflection only. It does not diagnose, treat, prevent, or cure back pain.
